title: Imperial warbot
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Youmay|the treaded [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]]|the bipedal [[warbot]]}} {{DroidSeries |image=[[File:Thatwarbot.jpg]] |name=Imperial warbot |creator= |manufacturer= |designer= |homeworld= |line= |model= |class= |degree= |cost= |length= |width= |height= |mass= |gender= |sensor= |plating= |armament=[[Blaster cannon/Legends|Blaster cannons]][[Star Wars (1977) 47|''Star Wars'' (1977) 47]] |equipment= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] **[[Z-X3's faction]] *[[Kligson's faction]] |firstmade= |retired= }} {{Quote|Here's what you've been sent to see, Commander."
"The new model warbot the Empire threw at your ground troops on Xeron...!|A Rebel trooper and Luke Skywalker|}} '''Imperial warbots''' were huge [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] released to supplement [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] ground forces following the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]. Although not considered as menacing as [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-ATs]], they were nonetheless deadly—warbots played a crucial role in the [[Battle of Xeron]], and were capable of fighting many foes at once using its [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]]. One damaged unit fell into the hands of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], and was later unleashed against Imperial-aligned [[Droid/Legends|droids]] on [[Kligson's Moon/Legends|Kligson's Moon]]. ==Characteristics== {{Quote|They're not in the same class as those AT-ATs you and General Rieekan's people faced on Hoth... but it's no picnic fighting 'em!|A Rebel trooper|}} A [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Imperial warbot resembled a mobile turret, with treaded wheels, two clawed arms, and a rotating head equipped with [[Blaster cannon/Legends|blaster cannons]]. Several times taller than the average [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], the warbots packed a significant punch, although [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] troops did not believe them as fearsome as the [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transports]] that pounded the Rebels on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. Imperial warbots were far from invulnerable, and the skilled [[Technician/Legends|technician]] [[Kligson/Legends|Kligson]] scorned what he saw as their shoddy craftsmanship, although they could be repaired rather simply using parts from similar machines. ==History== {{Quote|A product of the Empire...! Typical cheapjack craftsmanship! The diagram indicates proper reconstruction—but this kind of product isn't worth the effort. Most Imperial handiwork isn't.|Kligson|}} [[File:Warbott.jpg|thumb|left|200px|A warbot crushes Z-X3's forces]] A creation of the Imperial war effort in the months after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], Imperial warbots were in use against Rebel forces as early as [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], playing an important role in the [[Battle of Xeron]]. The effect they had against Rebel ground troops on [[Xeron]] brought them to the attention of the Rebel brass, which made capturing an intact specimen a priority. A damaged warbot fell into the hands of Rebel [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] not long after, prompting Skywalker to seek out the famed reclusive technician Kligson, whom he hoped could examine the droid and provide a full schematic. Kligson, a [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] who oversaw a community of [[Droid/Legends|droids]] on the "Droid World" [[Kligson's Moon/Legends|Kligson's Moon]], accepted the offer on the condition that he be allowed to repair and keep the machine. However, Kligson wasn't the only one with designs on the warbot. Although the cyborg had kept his moon officially out of the Galactic Civil War, his chief [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]], [[Z-X3|Zee-Exthree]], secretly led a [[Z-X3's faction|faction]] of Imperial-aligned droids that plotted to overthrow Kligson and deliver the "Droid World" to the Empire. Zee-Exthree also had come into possession of a damaged warbot, and hoped to use Skywalker's droid to repair their model and unleash it in their coup. However, they were outwitted and defeated by Kligson in the ensuing [[Kligson's Moon civil war|civil war]], after the cyborg rebuilt Skywalker's droid and utterly destroyed Zee-Exthree's faction. ==Behind the scenes== The Imperial warbot first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 47|''Star Wars'' (1977) 47]], written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and released in [[1981]]. In the comic, the warbot was illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]]. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (1977) 47|''Star Wars'' (1977) 47]] {{Reprint|Star Wars (1977) 47}} {{1st}} *''[[The Further Adventures: Droid World]]'' {{C|as Imperial Warbot}} ==Sources== *{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 8: Tales of the Clone Wars}} {{1stID|Imperial warbot}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Battle droid models]] [[Category:Droid models of the Galactic Empire]]
